item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are no circumstances where the bury your gays trope (or, in this case, the "bury your attempted queer family" trope) can be something I'll ever be comfortable with. Portraying the hardships of queer existence without providing any true resolution (especially when it's not being written from a queer perspective) turns the narrative into a shallow, meaningless misery that lacks a real message beyond "queer existence hard," a message I already get every single day of my life.

It's meaningful for LGBTQ+ people to describe the personal hardships they face; it's meaningful for anyone to write stories where, despite the odds, us queer folk are able to survive and be happy; it's not meaningful to write stories where our hardship is a cheap, emotionally exploitative punch that requires no effort to accomplish.

-1

Vote reversed following updates to the article. This is much better now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 100 points to the first person to understand the double meaning in the title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, but there was a missing 'y'. I've fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
syndactly,

syndactyly?

Sorry, both are right? I just found 'syndactyly' instead of 'syndactly'. ;_;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've reread this today and it has to be a -1 from me. The build-up is interesting and creates enough suspense to feel like it has stakes and story to it. However, the last addendum breaks my suspension of disbelief almost entirely, and ruins pretty much all atmosphere that was built before it. The characters themselves seem pretty bland since there isn't much development to them besides their odd smug.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this would have had an impact on me if there was as much attention paid to developing the characters as there was hinting at the story behind them.

I don't feel anything for any of the characters involved.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I'm barredowl! I will be performing a review on behalf of the Site Crit Team today.

[+] Show review
[-] Hide review

General

There's some details I'm iffy about in this, particularly because I think they could have been executed a little better. The environmental storytelling in this, for example, still feels a little hamfisted for my tastes, and some details (such as the menstrual blood) could have either been reordered to the bottom or removed and give the reader some time to let them come to the conclusion here. In addition, I'm not too sure about the discovery log: I feel like some parts of it feel too extraneous and could easily be integrated with the first update log without problem, so it being its own thing is kinda strange.

Overall, though, I think I generally enjoyed the romance story put down here, and I definitely got a lot out of this piece in general, but there are still some issues which I think could be sorted out later on; for now, this is a tentative upvote.

Edit: After some consideration, due to how little the actual characters were developed and expounded upon, I have changed this vote to a downvote.

Line-By-Line

Fourteen SCP-4724-A instances were recovered, but all expired within a month, due to congenital defects, strokes, and a lack of proper digestive tracts.

The emboldened comma to help the sentence flow should be removed.

The two approach the freezer in which SCP-4724-N is held, POI-7484's arm fully regenerated.

While this is technically correct, I found myself getting a little confused by how this sentence in particular was phrased. I would personally add the word "with" after the comma to clarify the dependent clause here.

That's all from me. Thank you for your time!

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to barredowl or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't realize this was a contest entry! You threaded it all together beautifully. The root cellar is still getting to me too!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to all the people in irc who looked over this as it was developing, and a big thanks to the authors who helped me, especially Mew-ltiverse, A Random Day, and especially Voct for the title and NatVoltaic for helping me revamp the ending.

Source of the pic here.
